negative_cases.why_wrong: Kcl Inductor Idt Voltage: forces the branch-current integral to zero, so it compiles and runs but misses every nonzero current-integration sample.
Kcl Inductor Idt Voltage: uses twice the required inductance scale, preserving waveform shape but failing the calibrated integral magnitude.
Kcl Inductor Idt Voltage: uses the correct V(p,n) contribution target but negates the branch-current integral, producing the opposite-polarity response.
Kcl Inductor Idt Voltage: uses a nonzero idt initial condition, adding a large DC offset while retaining the later current-driven slope.
Kcl Inductor Idt Voltage: uses 0.8x of the required inductance scale, so coarse shape checks pass but magnitude-sensitive samples fail.
